name: AI Content Idea Generator

objects.role: Entry-point service object that monitors the Airtable Content Ideas base Submissions table for new idea records and forwards them into the system.
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming submission events including the idea text and record metadata received from Airtable.
objects.behavior: When a new idea record is created in the Airtable Content Ideas Submissions table, forward the idea content and record ID to the idea-expansion peer.

peers.relationship: Forward the new idea text and Airtable record ID when a new submission event arrives

event_sources: webhook: Airtable record created in Content Ideas / Submissions table

objects.role: Business logic object that applies AI-powered content ideation rules to generate additional and related content ideas from a submitted seed idea.
objects.state_description: Tracks the current seed idea being processed and the set of AI-generated related ideas produced for it.
objects.behavior: When a new idea arrives, use the generate-ideas skill to produce a set of additional and related content ideas based on the submitted seed. Compile the generated ideas into a structured list ready for storage. Send the generated ideas along with the original record ID to the submissions-store peer.

peers.relationship: Send the AI-generated ideas and the original Airtable record ID for storage

objects.role: Write service object that records AI-generated ideas back into the Airtable Content Ideas base Submissions table.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all AI-generated idea sets written back to Airtable, keyed by original submission record ID.
objects.behavior: When a set of AI-generated ideas and a record ID are received, write the ideas into the corresponding record in the Airtable Content Ideas Submissions table.

steps: The workflow starts when a new content idea is submitted as a new record in Airtable, using that creation event as the trigger to begin idea expansion.

After the trigger fires, the workflow retrieves the submitted idea from Airtable and carries forward both the idea content and the corresponding record reference so the results can be written back to the same submission.
The submitted idea is then sent to ChatGPT to generate additional and related content ideas, and the output is organized into a structured list based on the original seed idea.
Once the related ideas have been generated, the workflow writes the AI-produced ideas back into Airtable on the corresponding submission record associated with the original idea.

modifications.intent: For those really short seed ideas, just keep it pretty minimal instead of spinning out a big list.

name: Gong Sales Call AI Coach

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives incoming call data and transcripts from Gong via webhook
objects.state_description: Logs of received webhook payloads including call metadata, transcript, rep info, and manager info
objects.behavior: When a webhook payload arrives from Gong, forward the full call data (transcript, rep identity, manager identity, call metadata) to the sales-coaching-analyzer peer.

peers.relationship: Forward full Gong call payload (transcript, rep, manager, metadata) when a new call webhook arrives

objects.role: Business logic object that analyzes sales call transcripts against a rubric, scores them, and routes feedback to the appropriate outputs
objects.state_description: Tracks analyzed calls with their scores, generated feedback, rep identity, manager identity, and routing decisions
objects.behavior: When a call payload arrives, analyze the transcript against the sales call effectiveness rubric covering dimensions such as discovery, objection handling, next steps, rapport, and value articulation — score the call out of 30. Generate insightful, actionable feedback summarizing strengths and areas for improvement. If the score is below 15, flag the call for manager escalation. Send the feedback record to the call-feedback-log write service. Send a feedback summary Slack message to the sales-rep-notifier. If the score is below 15, also send the summarized feedback to the sales-manager-notifier.

peers.relationship: Send structured feedback record (rep name, call date, score, full feedback) for storage
Send summarized feedback and score to notify the rep
Send summarized feedback, score, and rep identity to notify the manager when call score is below 15

objects.role: Write service object that stores call feedback records in the Sales Coaching Google Sheet tab Call Feedback Log
objects.state_description: Append-only log of all stored feedback records including rep name, call date, score, and full feedback text
objects.behavior: When a feedback record is received, append it as a new row to the 'Call Feedback Log' tab of the 'Sales Coaching' Google Sheet.

objects.role: Write service object that posts feedback summaries to the #sales-coaching Slack channel for the sales rep
objects.state_description: Log of Slack messages sent to reps with their feedback summaries and scores
objects.behavior: When a feedback summary is received, post it to the #sales-coaching Slack channel, addressing the message to the specific rep and including their score and actionable feedback summary.

objects.role: Write service object that posts escalated feedback summaries to the rep's Sales Manager via Slack when a call scores below 15
objects.state_description: Log of Slack escalation messages sent to managers including rep identity, score, and summarized feedback
objects.behavior: When an escalation message is received, post a direct Slack message to the rep's Sales Manager in the #sales-coaching Slack channel or as a direct message, including the rep's name, call score, and summarized feedback requiring attention.

steps: When Gong sends a webhook for a completed sales call, the workflow receives the full call payload, including the transcript, the sales rep’s identity, the manager’s identity, and relevant call metadata.

The workflow analyzes the Gong transcript against a sales call effectiveness rubric covering discovery, objection handling, next steps, rapport, and value articulation, then scores the call out of 30 and generates actionable feedback highlighting strengths and improvement areas. If the score is below 15, the call is marked for manager escalation.
The workflow stores the resulting feedback record in Google Sheets so the call’s score and coaching notes are available for future reference.
The workflow sends the sales rep a Slack message summarizing their call feedback, including their score and actionable coaching guidance.
If the call scores below 15 out of 30, the workflow also sends the summarized feedback in Slack to the rep’s manager so they can review and follow up.

modifications.intent: Stop sending summarized feedback to sales-manager-notifier for any call score below 15 starting immediately. Continue analyzing every Gong call, continue appending the full feedback record to call-feedback-log, and continue sending the feedback summary to sales-rep-notifier exactly as before.

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives new lead submissions from the Jotform lead capture form embedded on the website.
objects.state_description: Logs each incoming lead submission with fields: name, company, company size, region, and submission timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a new lead submission arrives from Jotform, forward the full lead payload (name, company, company size, region) to the lead-routing business logic object.

peers.relationship: Forward full lead payload when a new lead submission arrives

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives meeting-booked and closed-won-deal events from HubSpot CRM.
objects.state_description: Logs each incoming CRM event with type (meeting-booked or closed-won), associated rep, deal/meeting details, and event timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a meeting-booked or closed-won-deal event arrives from HubSpot, forward the event type and associated representative details to the ranking-update business logic object.

peers.relationship: Forward CRM event type and rep details when a meeting-booked or closed-won-deal event arrives

objects.role: Dual-role service object representing the Retool agent claim page that displays claim links and receives claim actions from sales representatives.
objects.state_description: Logs each claim action with rep name, lead identifier, and claim timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a claim link is generated, make it accessible on the claim page. When a sales representative submits a claim action on the Retool app, forward the claim details (rep name, lead identifier, claim timestamp) to the lead-routing business logic object.

peers.relationship: Forward claim details (rep name, lead identifier, claim timestamp) when a rep submits a claim action

event_sources: user action: sales rep claims lead via Retool app

objects.role: Business logic object that applies routing rules to assign leads to qualified sales representatives based on region and company size, and processes claim actions.
objects.state_description: Tracks active leads with their routing criteria (region, company size), assigned representative, claim status, and claim timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a new lead arrives, evaluate the region (Northeast, Southeast, Midwest, West) and company size (under 500 employees or 500 and above) to determine the qualified sales representative. Send the lead details and a claim link to the slack-notifications write service. When a claim action arrives from the Retool claim app, record the rep name and claim timestamp, then send the updated lead record (rep name, claim timestamp, lead status set to claimed) to the sales-pipeline write service.

peers.relationship: Send lead details and claim link when a new lead is routed to a qualified rep
Send updated lead record with rep name, claim timestamp, and claimed status when a rep claims a lead

objects.role: Business logic object that processes HubSpot CRM events and computes updated ranking scores for sales representatives.
objects.state_description: Tracks current ranking scores per sales representative and the events that contributed to each score change.
objects.behavior: When a meeting-booked event arrives, increment the associated representative's ranking score by the meeting-booked weight. When a closed-won-deal event arrives, increment the associated representative's ranking score by the closed-won weight. Send the updated ranking score record to the sales-pipeline write service.

peers.relationship: Send updated rep ranking score when a meeting-booked or closed-won-deal event is processed

objects.role: Write service object that delivers Slack DM notifications to qualified sales representatives with lead details and claim links.
objects.state_description: Logs each Slack DM sent with recipient rep, lead details, claim link, and send timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a notification request arrives, send a Slack DM to the specified sales representative's Slack account containing the lead details (name, company, company size, region) and the claim link.

objects.role: Write service object that records and updates lead data in the Sales Pipeline Google Sheet, tab Active Leads.
objects.state_description: Logs all writes made to the Active Leads tab including rep name, claim timestamp, lead status, and rep ranking score updates.
objects.behavior: When a claim update arrives, write the rep name, claim timestamp, and lead status to the corresponding lead row in the Active Leads tab of the Sales Pipeline Google Sheet. When a ranking score update arrives, write the updated ranking score for the specified representative to the Active Leads tab of the Sales Pipeline Google Sheet.

steps: When a prospect submits a lead capture form on the website through Jotform, the workflow receives the full submission details, including the prospect’s name, company, company size, and region, for downstream processing.

The workflow evaluates each new lead using routing rules based on region (Northeast, Southeast, Midwest, or West) and company size (under 500 employees or 500 and above) to determine which sales representative is qualified to receive it.

When a lead matches a representative’s routing criteria, the workflow sends that representative a Slack direct message containing the lead details—name, company, company size, and region—along with a link they can use to claim the lead.
After receiving the Slack notification, an eligible sales representative claims the lead through the claim page provided in Retool.
When a representative submits a claim in Retool, the workflow records the representative’s name and claim timestamp, marks the lead as claimed, and updates the lead record accordingly.

When HubSpot CRM sends a meeting-booked or closed-won-deal event, the workflow increases the associated representative’s ranking score using the appropriate weight for that event type and records the updated score.

name: Zendesk-HubSpot Sales Opportunity Scout

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives new support ticket events from Zendesk and forwards them into the pipeline
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming ticket events including ticket ID, subject, body, tags, customer email, and metadata
objects.behavior: When a new support ticket arrives, forward the full ticket payload (ticket ID, subject, body, tags, customer email, requester details) to the sales-opportunity-qualifier peer. When an internal note write request is received, call the Zendesk API to add the note to the specified ticket.

peers.relationship: Forward full ticket payload when a new support ticket arrives

objects.role: Read service that retrieves customer and company information from HubSpot by email or contact ID
objects.state_description: No persistent state — retrieves data on demand from HubSpot
objects.behavior: Answers queries about customer records including company name, company size, account type, email domain, and CRM relationship history. When answering queries, call the `hubspot_customers_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that evaluates support tickets for sales opportunity signals and qualifying criteria
objects.state_description: Tracks tickets under evaluation, their qualification status, extracted signals, and HubSpot customer data retrieved during analysis
objects.behavior: When a ticket payload arrives, first analyze the ticket tags for explicit signals of sales interest or enterprise inquiries (e.g., tags like 'pricing', 'enterprise', 'upgrade', 'capacity'). Then query hubspot-customers for the customer's CRM record using the requester email. With the HubSpot data in hand, evaluate whether the customer meets all qualifying thresholds: company size of 200+ employees, account type of Business or Enterprise, a non-free-tier email domain (not gmail.com, yahoo.com, hotmail.com, etc.), and ticket content referencing pricing or capacity limits. If the customer clearly meets all thresholds and explicit sales signals are present, classify as a qualified opportunity. If sales interest remains unclear after the explicit signal check, analyze subtle indicators: mentions of enterprise demos, account consolidation, or high usage patterns in the ticket body. If subtle indicators are present alongside partial qualification criteria, still classify as a qualified opportunity. When a qualified opportunity is identified, send the opportunity record to sales-pipeline-sheet and send an internal note request to zendesk-tickets simultaneously. If no qualifying signals or criteria are met, discard the ticket with no further action.

peers.relationship: Query for customer CRM record by requester email when evaluating a ticket
Send qualified opportunity record with ticket details, customer data, qualification reasoning, and signals when an opportunity is identified
Send internal note content and ticket ID when an opportunity is identified so a note is added to the ticket

objects.role: Write service that records qualified sales opportunities into the Sales Pipeline Google Sheet under the Qualified Opportunities tab
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all opportunity records written to the sheet, including ticket ID, customer name, company size, account type, qualification signals, and timestamp
objects.behavior: When an opportunity record is received, append a new row to the 'Qualified Opportunities' tab of the 'Sales Pipeline' Google Sheet containing ticket ID, customer name, company name, company size, account type, email domain, ticket summary, qualification signals, subtle indicators if any, and evaluation timestamp.

steps: When a new support ticket is created in Zendesk, the workflow receives the full ticket details, including the ticket ID, subject, message body, tags, customer email, and requester information, so the ticket can be evaluated for possible sales follow-up.

The workflow reviews the Zendesk ticket tags for explicit sales-related signals, such as pricing, enterprise, upgrade, or capacity-related tags, to determine whether the request shows clear commercial intent.

The workflow looks up the requester in HubSpot using the customer's email address to retrieve current customer and company context, including company name, company size, account type, email domain, and CRM relationship history.

Using the Zendesk ticket and HubSpot record together, the workflow checks whether the customer matches the sales qualification rules: company size of at least 200 employees, an account type of Business or Enterprise, a non-free email domain, and ticket content that mentions pricing or capacity limits.

If explicit sales intent is still not clear, the workflow performs a second-pass review of the Zendesk ticket body for softer signals of opportunity, including requests for enterprise demos, account consolidation, or signs of high usage; if those signals appear together with some qualifying customer attributes, the ticket can still be treated as a sales opportunity.

When the ticket is classified as a qualified sales opportunity, the workflow records the opportunity in a sales pipeline spreadsheet and adds an internal note to the Zendesk ticket summarizing the qualification; if the ticket does not meet the qualifying conditions, no further action is taken.

modifications.intent: Only treat a support ticket as a qualified opportunity when it includes explicit sales-interest tags like pricing, enterprise, upgrade, or capacity; if those tags are missing, do not qualify it based on subtle enterprise signals alone.

events.input: A new support ticket has arrived in Zendesk. Ticket ID: TKT-8821, Subject: 'Exploring enterprise options and pricing for our team', Body: 'Hi, we are currently hitting capacity limits on our current plan and are evaluating options for scaling up. We have around 350 employees and would love to understand your enterprise pricing and whether a demo could be arranged for our leadership team. We are also looking at consolidating multiple accounts into one enterprise agreement.', Tags: ['pricing', 'enterprise', 'upgrade', 'capacity'], Customer Email: 'daniel.marsh@acmecorp.com', Requester: Daniel Marsh, IT Director at Acme Corp.

expect.action: A qualified sales opportunity is recorded in the sales pipeline spreadsheet for Zendesk ticket TKT-8821, tied to Daniel Marsh at Acme Corp, summarizing enterprise pricing interest, upgrade/capacity needs, demo request, and account consolidation interest. An internal note is added to Zendesk ticket TKT-8821 stating that the ticket qualified for sales follow-up based on explicit sales tags, Acme Corp’s 350-employee size, Business plan status, non-free domain acmecorp.com, and pricing/capacity signals confirmed with HubSpot context for contact CT-100482 and company CO-20871.
expect.reason: The ticket meets the baseline qualification rules because it has explicit sales-related tags, pricing and capacity intent in the ticket content, and HubSpot shows a Business customer at a 350-employee company with a non-free domain.

events.input: A new support ticket has arrived in Zendesk. Ticket ID: TKT-9104, Subject: 'Need a demo and enterprise pricing for a larger rollout', Body: 'Hi team, we are expanding usage across departments and have around 280 employees. We are hitting plan limits, want to understand enterprise pricing, and would like to schedule a demo for our operations leadership. We are also considering consolidating several workspaces under one agreement.', Tags: ['pricing', 'enterprise', 'capacity'], Customer Email: 'melissa.choi@northbridgeops.com', Requester: Melissa Choi, Operations Director at Northbridge Ops.

expect.action: No sales pipeline entry is created and no internal Zendesk qualification note is added for ticket TKT-9104.
expect.reason: The ticket shows explicit sales intent in Zendesk, but there is no matching HubSpot customer/company context in the provided reference data to verify the required qualification attributes, so the workflow does not qualify it for sales follow-up.

events.input: A new support ticket has arrived in Zendesk. Ticket ID: TKT-9105, Subject: 'Questions about scaling our account setup', Body: 'Hello, our company has roughly 420 employees and we are reviewing whether an enterprise arrangement would make sense. We would be interested in a demo for leadership and may need to combine multiple teams into one managed setup as usage continues to grow.', Tags: ['account-management', 'team-setup'], Customer Email: 'robert.king@alturasystems.com', Requester: Robert King, IT Program Manager at Altura Systems.

expect.action: [suppressed by M001] No sales pipeline entry is created and no internal Zendesk qualification note is added for ticket TKT-9105, even though the ticket body contains softer enterprise-opportunity signals such as demo interest and account consolidation.
expect.reason: M001 applies because the ticket lacks the required explicit sales-interest tags pricing, enterprise, upgrade, or capacity, so subtle enterprise signals alone cannot qualify it.
